D3 Chemistry L1 Identify Reference

Why Things Have Colour — Absorb · Reflect · Transmit

white light hits an object; the object splits the spectrum into three buckets — absorbed (becomes heat), reflected (what we see if opaque), transmitted (what we see if transparent)

Every coloured object follows the same energy balance: incident light = absorbed + reflected + transmitted. For an opaque object only absorbed + reflected matter; for a transparent one, transmitted vs absorbed. The wavelengths that survive (reflect or transmit) are the colour you see; the wavelengths absorbed warm the object.

Pick an object class. Three bars show the wavelength-resolved absorbance / reflectance / transmittance; the final swatch is what the eye perceives.
